sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: The upcoming clash between Crystal Palace and Nottingham Forest on May 5, 2025, at 19:00 is set to be an intriguing encounter at Selhurst Park in London. Crystal Palace, currently sitting in 12th position with 45 points, will host Nottingham Forest, who are impressively placed 6th with 60 points in the league standings. This Round 35 fixture, officiated by Andy Madley, who averages 3.1 yellow cards and 0.11 red cards per game, promises to be a competitive battle. Given the league positions, Forest enter as slight favorites, but Palace will look to leverage their home advantage in this matchup.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at their last meeting on October 21, 2024, Nottingham Forest edged out Crystal Palace with a tight 1-0 victory at home, with Matz Sels earning Player of the Match honors with an 8.9 Sofascore rating. Over the last 5 matchups between these sides, Forest have dominated with 2 wins, while 3 games ended in draws, leaving Palace without a victory in this fixture recently. Head-to-head trends show a consistent pattern of low-scoring games, with 6 out of 6 matches having less than 2.5 goals, and 8 out of 10 matches seeing fewer than 4.5 yellow cards. Additionally, 4 out of 5 recent encounters had less than 10.5 corners, suggesting a match with limited set-piece action.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Crystal Palace
sections.text: For Crystal Palace, Eberechi Eze stands out with his flair, ranking 5th in the league for successful dribbles per game at 2.0, alongside 4 goals and 8 assists this season. Jean-Philippe Mateta has been a key attacking threat with 14 goals, overperforming his expected goals (xG) of 12.11 by 1.89. Ismaïla Sarr, in form over the last 5 matches with a 7.08 Sofascore rating, 2 goals, and 2 assists, will also be crucial. Defensively, Marc Guéhi, with 3 goals and 2 assists, provides stability with a solid 7.07 average rating. Players like Daniel Muñoz, contributing 4 goals and 4 assists, will also play a vital role in midfield.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Nottingham Forest
sections.text: Nottingham Forest boast firepower up front with Chris Wood, who ranks 4th in the league for goals scored with an impressive 19, massively overperforming his xG of 11.40 by 7.6. Morgan Gibbs-White has been a creative force in midfield with 5 goals, 7 assists, and a 7.2 average rating. Anthony Elanga adds dynamism with 6 goals and 9 assists, while Murillo, a standout in recent matches against Palace with a 7.47 average Sofascore rating over 3 starts, anchors the defense. Nikola Milenković, with 4 goals from defense, will also be a player to watch in both boxes.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Statistics - Crystal Palace
sections.text: Crystal Palace come into this game with a streak of no losses in their last 3 matches, showing resilience. Their home games have seen over 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 6 outings, indicating potential for an open game. Defensively, they've kept 10 clean sheets this season, ranking 6th in the league, and excel in tackles (20.56 per game, 3rd) and interceptions (8.53 per game, 3rd). They've scored 43 goals, ranking 14th, but impressively overperform their xG by 9.18, the best in the league. However, their 42.74% average ball possession (17th) and 77.45% accurate passes (20th) suggest struggles in controlling games.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Statistics - Nottingham Forest
sections.text: Nottingham Forest have failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 5 away matches, a concern heading into Selhurst Park. They've also been the first to concede in 4 of their last 5 away games, hinting at early vulnerabilities. Their matches tend to be low-scoring on the road, with 5 of 7 having under 2.5 goals. Offensively, they score late, with 24.39% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. While specific team stats for Forest are limited in this dataset, their higher league position (6th) and points tally (60) reflect a strong campaign.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Game Dynamics
sections.text: Crystal Palace’s strength lies in ground duels, winning 51.67% (4th in the league), and their high pressing, winning possessions in the final third at a rate of 3.91 per game (10th). They also score significantly in the latter stages, with 17.02% of goals between the 76th and 90th minutes. Nottingham Forest, meanwhile, distribute goals more evenly across periods but peak early (17.07% in the first 15 minutes) and late (24.39% after the 76th minute). With both teams tending towards low corner counts (Palace at 4.59 per game, 12th; recent H2H trends under 10.5 corners), set-pieces may not dominate. Palace’s high foul count (374, 15th) could test referee Andy Madley’s card average of 3.1 per game.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Streaks
sections.text: Nottingham Forest hold a remarkable streak of no losses in 9 head-to-head matches against Crystal Palace, showcasing their historical edge. Crystal Palace have hit the woodwork 10 times this season, ranking 13th among the unluckiest teams in the league. Palace also rank 2nd for the percentage of long passes in total passes at 10.03%, indicating a direct style that could contrast with Forest’s approach. Additionally, 74.42% of Palace’s goals have been assisted, ranking 7th, showing strong teamwork in attack. Both teams’ recent games suggest a low corner count, with 6 of 7 Palace home matches and 5 of 5 Forest away matches under 10.5 corners.
